name: District of Columbia v. Heller
summary: The Supreme Court held that the Second Amendment protects an individual's right to possess firearms for lawful purposes, particularly self-defense in the home, while suggesting some longstanding regulatory measures remain valid.
significance: Heller first recognized an individual right to keep and bear arms under the Second Amendment, and its dicta noting that certain 'presumptively lawful' regulations — including prohibitions on possession by felons and the mentally ill — inform the debate over whether similar categorical bans on drug users are constitutional.
